intro: Polycoryne fernandensis is a plant species belonging to the Polycoryne genus, a type of flowering plant native to tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the management of infections. The bioactive constituents of Polycoryne fernandensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, fever, and respiratory problems.
